patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 00/13] mm: PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs

version_history.subject: [PATCH 00/13] mm: PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs

series_items.subject: [PATCH 01/13] mm: add softleaf_to_pmd() and convert existing callers
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function called `softleaf_to_pmd()` which is the PMD counterpart to `softleaf_to_pte()`. It completes the symmetry of the softleaf abstraction for page table leaf entries. The existing callers of `swp_entry_to_pmd()` are converted to use `softleaf_to_pmd()` instead, keeping the feature patches focused on new functionality rather than refactoring code. This change is necessary for upcoming PMD swap entry support which needs to construct PMD entries from swap entries.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 02/13] mm: extract ensure_on_mmlist() helper
series_items.patch_summary: This patch extracts a helper function called ensure_on_mmlist() from the mm module, which ensures that an mm_struct is added to the init_mm.mmlist so that swapoff can find and unuse its swap entries. The double-checked locking pattern was previously open-coded in try_to_unmap_one() and copy_nonpresent_pte(). The helper function will be reused by upcoming PMD-level swap entry code paths. The insertion point of the mm_struct into the mmlist is irrelevant, as only membership matters for swapoff to find it.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 03/13] fs/proc: use softleaf_has_pfn() in pagemap PMD walker
series_items.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the pagemap PMD walker by adding a check to ensure that only non-present PMDs that encode a PFN are processed. The existing code assumed all non-present PMDs were migration entries, which would crash on newer PMD types. The fix uses the softleaf_has_pfn() function to guard the page lookup, matching how non-present PTEs are handled.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 04/13] mm/huge_memory: move softleaf_to_folio() inside migration branch
series_items.patch_summary: This patch moves the call to softleaf_to_folio() inside a migration branch in the change_non_present_huge_pmd() function, ensuring that it only occurs when a folio pointer is actually needed and valid. This prevents potential crashes caused by bogus pointers produced for swap entries. The approach taken is to conditionally call softleaf_to_folio() based on the type of entry being processed.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 05/13] mm: add PMD swap entry detection support
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds support for detecting PMD (Page Map Directory)-level swap entries in the Linux kernel. Currently, when a huge page is swapped out, it's split into 512 PTE-level (Page Table Entry) swap entries, which can lead to loss of huge page information across swap cycles. This patch introduces infrastructure to detect PMD-level swap entries and adds helpers to set and clear an exclusive overlay bit in these entries. The goal is to preserve huge page information during swap operations.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 06/13] mm: add PMD swap entry splitting support
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds support for splitting a PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries in the Linux kernel's memory management (mm) module. This is done by adding a new branch to the __split_huge_pmd_locked() function that handles PMD swap entries differently than migration splits, as swap entries point to swap slots rather than pages. The patch also updates the pmd_is_valid_softleaf() function to recognize swap entries as valid leaf entries. The changes are made to support partial-range mprotect and munmap operations, as well as PMD-COW fallbacks and swap-in/swap-off fallbacks. The patch aims to improve memory management efficiency by allowing for more fine-grained control over swap entry splitting.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 07/13] mm: handle PMD swap entries in fork path
series_items.patch_summary: This patch improves the handling of PMD (Page Map Directory) swap entries during the fork process in Linux. It introduces a new function `swap_dup_entries_direct` to duplicate a contiguous range of swap slots, and modifies the `copy_huge_non_present_pmd` function to preserve THP (Transparent Huge Pages) by duplicating PMD swap entries instead of splitting them. The patch also adds retry logic for table allocation in case of failure. This change mirrors the existing behavior for PTEs (Page Table Entries).

series_items.subject: [PATCH 08/13] mm: swap in PMD swap entries as whole THPs during swapoff
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds the ability to swap in PMD-level swap entries as whole THPs during swapoff, mirroring the existing behavior at PTE granularity. It introduces a new function `swapin_alloc_pmd_folio` that allocates and charges a PMD-sized swap folio, which is then swapped in using `swapin_folio`. If allocation or charging fails, the PMD swap entry is split into individual PTE-level entries via `__split_huge_pmd`. The patch also updates various functions to handle PMD swap entries correctly during swapoff.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 09/13] mm: handle PMD swap entries in non-present PMD walkers
series_items.patch_summary: This patch updates various Linux kernel modules to handle PMD (Page Map Directory) swap entries in non-present PMD walkers, mirroring the behavior of PTE (Page Table Entry) level equivalents. The changes include adding a new helper function `smaps_account_swap()` to account for swap and swap_pss in PMD paths, updating functions like `zap_huge_pmd()`, `change_non_present_huge_pmd()`, and `madvise_cold_or_pageout_pmd_range()` to handle swap entries correctly. The patch also fixes issues related to MADV_FREE, mbind(), and hmm_vma_handle_absent_pmd(). Overall, the goal is to ensure that PMD swap entries are handled consistently with PTE level equivalents, preventing bugs and unexpected behavior in various kernel modules.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 10/13] mm: handle PMD swap entries in UFFDIO_MOVE
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function, move_swap_pmd(), to handle PMD-level swap entries in the UFFDIO_MOVE operation. It allows moving whole PMD swap entries and re-anchors the swap-cache folio's anon rmap to the destination VMA. The patch also introduces a fallback mechanism for split swap-cache folios and preserves single-owner semantics by rejecting non-exclusive PMD swap entries.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 11/13] mm: handle PMD swap entry faults on swap-in
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function, do_huge_pmd_swap_page(), to handle PMD swap entry faults during swap-in operations. It resolves the entire 2 MB mapping in one shot by looking up the folio in the swap cache, allocating a PMD-order folio if necessary, and restoring soft_dirty and uffd_wp from the swap PMD. The patch also handles cases where PMD-order resources are unavailable, splitting the PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries if necessary. Additionally, it updates vmf->orig_pmd and calls wp_huge_pmd() to COW immediately when a read-only PMD is faulted with a write operation.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 12/13] mm: install PMD swap entries on swap-out
series_items.patch_summary: This patch improves the handling of huge page mappings when swapping out memory. When a huge page is swapped out, it was previously split into 512 smaller pages, losing the original mapping and requiring khugepaged to rebuild it later. This patch introduces PMD-level swap entries, which can be used instead of individual PTEs for each slot in the huge page. The patch adds new functions to install and manage these PMD-level swap entries, mirroring the existing PTE swap-out path at PMD granularity. This change reduces overhead and improves performance by preserving the original mapping across the swap round-trip.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 13/13] selftests/mm: add PMD swap entry tests
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds tests for PMD-level swap entries in the Linux kernel. The tests cover various scenarios, including swapping out and in a THP (Transparent Huge Page), fork operations, write faults, munmap, mprotect, mremap, pagemap reading, MADV_FREE, UFFDIO_MOVE, and swapoff with active PMD swap entries. These tests aim to verify that the kernel correctly handles PMD-level swap entries and ensures data integrity in different scenarios.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This series of patches introduces PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs, allowing huge mappings to be preserved across the swap round-trip and reducing memory usage when swapping. The existing PTE-split path is still used as a fallback in cases where PMD-order resources are unavailable. This change has benefits including faster fault handling on swap-in, reduced memory allocation during swap, and improved performance for walkers such as zap and mprotect.

review_comments.author: Usama Arif (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern about the implementation of PMD swap entries, specifically the conversion of swp_entry_to_pmd() callers to softleaf_to_pmd(). The author agreed that this refactoring is necessary to keep feature patches focused on new functionality and not mix it with existing code. A prep patch will be added to make these changes before introducing the new PMD swap entry support.

review_comments.author: Usama Arif (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern about the swapoff path needing to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(). They moved the double-checked locking pattern into ensure_on_mmlist() in mm/internal.h and converted both callers, agreeing that this change is necessary for upcoming PMD-level swap entry code paths.

review_comments.author: Usama Arif (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern that pagemap_pmd_range_thp() will crash on non-present PMD-level swap entries, explaining that it assumes every non-present PMD is a migration entry and unconditionally calls softleaf_to_page(). The author proposed adding a guard with softleaf_has_pfn(), matching how pte_to_pagemap_entry() handles non-present PTEs.

review_comments.author: Usama Arif (author)
review_comments.summary: The author addressed a concern that change_non_present_huge_pmd() would crash on mprotect() when encountering a PMD swap entry by moving the call to softleaf_to_folio() into the migration_write branch, ensuring it only uses the folio pointer in that specific case.
review_comments.raw_body:
change_non_present_huge_pmd() calls softleaf_to_folio() unconditionally
at the top of the function.  softleaf_to_folio() extracts a PFN from
the entry and converts it to a folio pointer, which is only meaningful
for migration and device_private entries that encode a real PFN.

A swap entry encodes a swap offset instead, so softleaf_to_folio()
would produce a bogus pointer and crash on mprotect() when a PMD swap
entry is present.

Move the call into the migration_write branch where the folio is
actually used, so the function is safe for any non-present PMD type.

Signed-off-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
---
 mm/huge_memory.c | 3 ++-
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/mm/huge_memory.c b/mm/huge_memory.c
index 49da0746b8ca..d82a19b5e276 100644
--- a/mm/huge_memory.c
+++ b/mm/huge_memory.c
@@ -2619,11 +2619,12 @@ static void change_non_present_huge_pmd(struct mm_struct *mm,
 		bool uffd_wp_resolve)
 {
 	softleaf_t entry = softleaf_from_pmd(*pmd);
-	const struct folio *folio = softleaf_to_folio(entry);
 	pmd_t newpmd;
 
 	VM_WARN_ON(!pmd_is_valid_softleaf(*pmd));
 	if (softleaf_is_migration_write(entry)) {
+		const struct folio *folio = softleaf_to_folio(entry);
+
 		/*
 		 * A protection check is difficult so
 		 * just be safe and disable write

review_comments.author: Usama Arif (author)

The author also added checks to ensure that page and folio are left uninitialized in this branch, and that any future caller breaking the invariant is caught by VM_WARN_ON_ONCE(freeze).

review_comments.author: Usama Arif (author)

The author also added retry logic in case of allocation failures. A fix is planned.

review_comments.author: Kairui Song
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer Kairui Song thanked Usama Arif for his work, indicating a positive sentiment.

Reviewer Kairui Song noted that swap_map metadata can be reduced further by storing a swp_tb entry directly in ci->table, and suggested doing this later as part of cluster compaction.

Reviewer Kairui Song noted a potential conflict between this patch series and the swap folio allocation unification, specifically mentioning that the swap_cache_alloc_folio helper can be used to directly allocate 2M folios using PMD_ORDER. They suggested checking the linked patch for details and proposed updating their own series to disable forced order 0 fallback and allow callers to pass in a specific THP order.
review_comments.raw_body:
Hi Usama,

Thanks for the work!

---

swap_map is gone, metadata is still per slot but with PMD sized
swapout, I think soon we can store a swp_tb entry directly in
ci->table (make it a union maybe) so the metadata is significantly
reduced from there too. Better do that later with cluster compaction.

---

There is a slight conflict with the swap folio allocation unification,
which should be easy to solve. Just a little head up, check the
swap_cache_alloc_folio helper here:
https://lore.kernel.org/linux-mm/20260421-swap-table-p4-v3-4-2f23759a76bc@tencent.com/

We will be able to directly allocate 2M folios using
swap_cache_alloc_folio(orders = BIT(PMD_ORDER)) in the patch link
above. Might even help to avoid issues with splitting or raced swapin?

The conflict can be solved from either side, I'll update that series to
disable the forced order 0 fallback and let caller pass in (orders =
<mTHP order> | BIT(0)) instead.
